EDENBROOK ON SECOND AVE

CCN: 395397 · KINGSTON, PA 18704 · Luzerne County

Overview

Address
200 SECOND AVENUE, KINGSTON, PA 18704
Phone
5702889315
Certified beds
160
Avg daily residents
—
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1977-12-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Data not available.
— Not Available
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-06-06 10 7 4 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-08-23 43 12 34 248 1 248
Total weighted health score
113.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (62)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

61 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2026-01-21 2026-03-03
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-09-25 2025-11-04
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-09-25 2025-11-04
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-06-06 2025-07-16
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-06-06 2025-07-16
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2025-06-06 2025-07-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-06-06 2025-07-16
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-06-06 2025-07-16
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-06-06 2025-07-16
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2025-06-06 2025-07-16
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-17
0560 Protect a residents' right to refuse some types of non-requested transfers within the nursing home. D 2025-01-10 2025-01-29
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-01-10 2025-01-29
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-10
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-10
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0914 Provide bedrooms that don't allow residents to see each other when privacy is needed. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-24
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-05-15 2024-06-05
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-05-15 2024-06-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-15 2024-06-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-05-15 2024-06-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-15
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-15
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-15 2024-06-05
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-15
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. E 2024-03-15 2024-05-15
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-03-15 2024-06-05
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-03-15 2024-05-15
0807 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides drinks consistent with resident needs and preferences and sufficient to maintain resident hydration. E 2024-03-15 2024-05-15
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-09-22 2023-10-31
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. E 2023-09-22 2023-10-31
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-07-06 2023-07-31
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2023-07-06 2023-07-31
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2023-05-30 2023-07-06
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-05-30 2023-07-06
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. E 2023-05-30 2023-07-06
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2023-05-30 2023-07-06
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-05-30 2023-07-06
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2023-05-02 2023-07-06
